name: Billy Hart
abstract: Billy Hart (born November 29, 1940) is an American jazz drummer and educator. He is known internationally for his contributions with Herbie Hancock's "Mwandishi" band in the early 1970s, as well as with Shirley Horn, Stan Getz, and Quest, among many others.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: A Night of Genius-Artists Celebrate Mingus
An Evening with Lisa Hilton: Kaleidoscope

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: She had a band of me, Billy Hart and her. Did a lot of gigs together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Drummond
about_quotes.evidence: For a time Billy Hart and I used to play together in Kenny’s trio
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Drummond interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: David Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve always tried to have bands that do that... I stick with those guys because they’re great... Billy Hart used to tell me some drummer used to tell him... I had a conversation I had with Billy Hart. We had done two records
about_quotes.doc_title: David Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: "As far as I’m concerned, it’s Billy Hart... So we got – I got that right, which is good sideman money... Not when we’re making this kind of money, and not Billy Hart, Cecil McBee, or Rufus"
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I had Kenny Barron play piano, Chris White on bass, and Billy Hart on drums. We performed this concert in Attica prison
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Horn Shirley
about_quotes.evidence: I understand he apprenticed in your ensemble at seventeen. ... Walter Booker, Billy Hart were in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Horn Shirley — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
